== Notes for [[Item:Q118251|Q118251]] ==

Agatha de Normandie

d/o William the Conqueror and Mathilda de Flanders

From medlands:

-http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/ENGLAND,%20Kings%201066-1603.htm#Agathadiedbefore1074

8. AGATHE de Normandie (-before 1074, bur Bayeux Cathedral). Orderic Vitalis names “Agatham et Constantiam, Adelizam, Adelam et Ceciliam” as the daughters of King William and his wife[105]. According to William of Malmesbury, an unnamed daughter of King William was "affianced by messengers" to King Alfonso[106]. Orderic Vitalis identifies Agatha as the daughter who was betrothed to Harold (see above): he records that “Agatha regis filia”, who had previously been betrothed to “Heraldo”, was betrothed to “Amfurcio regi Galleciæ” but died before the journey and was buried “in ecclesia sanctæ Mariæ...in urbe Bajocensi”[107]. Matthew Paris places her as the fifth daughter (unnamed) betrothed to "Aldefonso Galiciæ regi", but different from the daughter betrothed to Harold[108]. Orderic says that she died en route to Spain, her body being brought back to Bayeux for burial[109]. The betrothal to Alfonso must have been a short-lived arrangement as he married his first wife in 1069[110].

Betrothed (by proxy Caen, Abbey of Holy Trinity [before 1069]) to ALFONSO VI King of Galicia and Leon, son of FERNANDO I King of Castile & his wife Infanta doña Sancha de Léon (Compostela [1037]-Toledo 30 Jun 1109, bur Sahagún, León, San Mancio chapel in the royal monastery of Santos Facundo y Primitivo). He succeeded in 1072 as ALFONSO VI King of Castile.

'''['''Betrothed ([after 1069]) to SIMON de Valois, son of RAOUL [III] “le Grand” Comte de Valois & his first wife Aelis de Bar-sur-Aube (-[30 Sep/1 Oct] 1080 Rome, bur 1082 Rome St Peter). The Vita Simonis records a ficitional speech of William I King of England in which he offers his (unnamed) daughter's hand to Simon, specifying that she had previously been betrothed to "regis Hispaniarum Anfursi et Roberti principis Apuliæ"[111]. The supposed betrothal to Robert of Apulia (which would have to refer to Robert "Guiscard" Duke of Apulia) is unrecorded in the numerous other sources dealing with his life and is probably pure fantasy. This does not instil confidence with respect to the historical accuracy of the whole passage, but if it is correct the daughter in question would presumably have been Agatha who was probably the daughter of King William betrothed to "Amfursio regi Galliciæ" (see above). Count Simon resigned his county in 1077, became a monk and went on pilgrimage to Rome where he died[112].''']'''

--------


Agatha, mentioned by Ordericus Vitalis as one of the daughters of William I of England and Matilda of Flanders, Agatha, who had been betrothed to Harold II (or his illegitimate namesake son), was afterwards demanded in marriage by Alphonso VI of Castile and delivered to his proxies to be conducted to him. But she, who had lost her former spouse who was to her liking, felt extreme repugnance to marry another. The Englishman she had seen and loved, but the Spaniard she was more averse to because she had never set eyes on him. She therefore, fervently prayed to the Almighty that she might never be carried into Spain, but that he would rather take her to himself. Her prayers were heard, and she died a virgin while she was on the road. Her corpse was brought back to and interred in Bayeux in 1079.

* The Ecclesiastical History of Orderic Vitalis - Orderic Vitalis

--------------------

Agatha was betrothed to Alfonso VI of Castile when she died at age 15.

--------------------

Rumored to have been betrothed to Harold. Another source states she 'died a virgin'.

Source: The book, 'Kings & Queens of Great Britain'
--------------------
m firstly ADELSINDIS, daughter of --- (-after 29 Apr 1050). "Malleni episcopi Gratianopolitani et Wigonis comitis et uxoris eius Adelsendis, filiorumque suorum Umberti et Wigonis" signed a charter dated 26 Aug 1035 relating to the donation of four churches in Vico to the abbey of Saint-Chaffre[2643]. According to Europäische Stammtafeln[2644], the first wife of Guigues [III] was "Gotelana de Clérieux". It appears that this hypothesis must be based on the charter dated [1090] under which "Wigo comes filius Gotelenne" donated property to Cluny[2645]. However, as noted above, a better interpretation of the documentation is that "Gotelenne" was the mother of Guigues [III] not his wife. A charter dated 29 Apr 1050 records the donation by "Guigoni…maiori, qui postea effectus est monachus…et Adelai comitissæ, et alii Guigoni, filio illius Guigonis…et uxori eius Petronillæ" to Saint-Pierre de Vienne[2646].

Ancestral File Number:<AFN> 8XJ0-4J

Ancestral File Number:<AFN> 8XJ0-4J

Line 2386 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/

Said to have been betrothed to Harold, Earl of Wessex.

_P_CCINFO 1-20792

She was bethrothed to Harold, Earl of Wessex and Alphonso, King ofGallicia.

She was bethrothed to Harold, Earl of Wessex and Alphonso, King of
Gallicia.

ALLEGED TO HAVE BEEN BETROTHED TO HAROLD GODWINSON

MENTIONED IN THE DOMESDAY BOOK

1 NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
1 NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
1 NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1171 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1215 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 972 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1571 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1152 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 667 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 3465 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1453 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 421 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1183 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 1284 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 182 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 182 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 182 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/
Line 421 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/

_P_CCINFO 1-20792
Name Suffix:<NSFX> Princess of Eng. Ancestral File Number:<AFN> 8XJ0-4J
Source: Butler.
Betrothed to Harold, Earl of Wessex, 1064.
Original individual @@P2203432695@@ (@@MS_NHFETTERLYFAMIL0@@) merged with @@P2203432553@@ (@@MS_NHFETTERLYFAMIL0@@)
Original individual @@P2203432695@@ (@@MS_NHFETTERLYFAMIL0@@) merged with @@P2203432746@@ (@@MS_NHFETTERLYFAMIL0@@)


1. Agatha was betrothed to Alfonso. Unknown GEDCOM info: MH:N94 Unknown GEDCOM info: D73ED306-4D3C-48B1-9B4F-DF50645A7890

_P_CCINFO 1-20792
Name Prefix:<NPFX> Princess of England Ancestral File Number:<AFN> 8XJ0-11

Line 2386 from GEDCOM File not recognizable or too long:
NAME Agatha Matilda Princess Of /ENGLAND/

She was mentioned in the Domesday Book.

She was betrothed to Harold Godwinsson and Alphonso of Leon, but became a nun.

She was betrothed to Harold Godwinsson and Alphonso of Leon, but became a nun.

Name Suffix:<NSFX> Of England

Name Suffix:<NSFX> Princess Of England

Mathilde var en engelsk prinsesse. Hun var annen gang gift med Vigo.


Betrothed to Alphonso, King of Gallicia.

Historian David Douglas says she was alleged to have been betrothed to Harold Godwinson and to Alphonzo of Leon.

Name Prefix:<NPFX> Princess Name Suffix:<NSFX> Of England

Agatha died without marrying before 1080, alleged to have been betroth ed to Harold Godwinson and to Alphonso of Leon.

[Kopi av ROYALS.FTW]

Was betrothed to King Harold.

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
